paper_title: Study of Ruminant Dentition in Neogene Basins of Calatayud and Rubielos de Mora

methods.description: This method involves the collection and classification of ruminant dental remains from specific Neogene basins in Central Spain. The purpose is to study the dentition of Andegameryx and Procervulus species from these regions.

steps.description: Select material from three different localities in the intramountain basins of the Iberian Chain, Central Spain.
steps.searchable_summary: select and collect ruminant dental samples from specified localities

steps.description: Recover material from Ágreda and Moratilla in the Calatayud basin, and Alto de Ballester 1 in the Rubielos de Mora basin.
Classify the species based on size and morphology, and assign nomenclature accordingly.
steps.searchable_summary: classify species and assign nomenclature based on morphology

steps.description: Analyze dental remains of Andegameryx and Procervulus from the collected samples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze dental remains for specified species
